actionUnits.condition: GvrReticlePointer (GameObject fileID 95) is child of Main Camera (fileID 135). This component exposes a single relevant public no-arg method SetAsMainPointer() to register the reticle as the active Gvr pointer. No UnityEvent m_Calls present; Awake/Start/Update are lifecycle-driven.

Verify MonoBehaviour id 896 on GvrControllerPointer (fileID 25) corresponds to script guid f3bf9c1bacbb04f72bb43e892c3a98a9 (GvrControllerVisualManager). Scene meta shows no UnityEvent m_Calls, no Collider, no Rigidbody, and no public no-arg methods. The script logic is guarded by UNITY_HAS_GOOGLEVR && (UNITY_ANDROID || UNITY_EDITOR) and toggles child visibility based on GvrController.State. To cover Start/Update and SetChildrenActive(true) path, simulate controller connected state: set GvrController.State = Connected before/during this action so Update observes the connected state and activates children (Transform children fileIDs 527 and 365).

Same component mapping as above (MonoBehaviour id 896 -> GvrControllerVisualManager). To cover the disconnect path and state-change branch in Update, simulate a transition to disconnected: set GvrController.State = Disconnected during this action so Update detects the change and invokes SetChildrenActive(false), deactivating children (fileIDs 527 and 365). Ensure the required compile symbols UNITY_HAS_GOOGLEVR and platform (UNITY_ANDROID or UNITY_EDITOR) are defined so code is active.

Map MonoBehaviour_0 id=915 on ddcontroller (fileID 68) to script guid aa952cbcc0eb13d4ca558b6da550ff55, which is the provided GvrControllerVisual. Scene meta shows its serialized fields wired as: touchPoint=fileID 109; material_idle=guid 3b4f6031d569a624ba87b631c93a788a; material_app=guid 3fe59d3fe0697b84196c28c184197906; material_system=guid 6c9fcd230d4724c40af9e27326dc25f1; material_touchpad=guid a89bc9f2db5f60743a95275d0336b6c2; touchTransparent=guid 089662c06964d614db131b6b344431e4; touchOpaque=guid 7bc1b31ae424a1448b1be6be3707ad31. This confirms the MonoBehaviour component in the scene is the exact settings for the attached script. Event to trigger for coverage: GvrArmModel.Instance.OnArmModelUpdate, which GvrControllerVisual subscribes to in Start(). Drive state over frames so OnArmModelUpdate executes all branches: t=0.0s ensure GvrArmModel.Instance exists and fires updates; all buttons false, not touching -> expect controllerRenderer uses material_idle, touchPoint active and scales down (Lerp to zero). t=0.2s set GvrController.IsTouching=true, TouchPos=(0.5,0.5) -> touchPoint active, scales up (Lerp to TOUCHPAD_POINT_DIMENSIONS) and moves to (-x, TOUCHPAD_POINT_Y_OFFSET - y, TOUCHPAD_POINT_ELEVATION). t=0.4s set GvrController.ClickButton=true -> expect material_touchpad, touchPoint deactivated. t=0.6s ClickButton=false; set GvrController.AppButton=true -> expect material_app, touchPoint active. t=0.8s AppButton=false; set GvrController.Recentering=true -> expect material_system. t=1.0s Recentering=false; set GvrController.IsTouching=false (verifies wasTouching reset and shrink animation). t=1.2s set GvrControllerVisual.AlphaValue=0.5 -> expect controllerRenderer/touchRenderer use PropertyBlock and touchTransparent; then set AlphaValue=1.0 -> expect touchOpaque. Note: Awake/Start are auto-invoked by Unity; OnDestroy coverage requires disabling/destroying ddcontroller or scene unload (not directly triggerable here).
